RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 203-B, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
State Representative Holly Jones, a Republican, represents part of St. Louis County (District 88) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2022.
In addition to her legislative duties, Jones works as the Business Manager for Renz Law LLC. Prior to being elected, Jones worked in global logistics and as a Civilian Contractor in Program Management for a global technology company specializing in contracts for the Air Force, Navy and DOD. Jones was honored as the youngest Program Manager to be awarded the coveted mentor protégé program through the DOD fulfilling their requirements for small business nationwide.
A graduate of Washington High School, Jones attended Southwest Missouri State University. She currently resides in Eureka Missouri.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Designates November 13th each year as "Believe in Gianna Day"
Establishes the "End Organ Harvesting Act of 2025"
Establishes provisions relating to of inducing the death of dogs or cats
Creates the "Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act"
Modifies provisions relating to pyramid sales schemes
Modifies provisions relating to abuse and neglect reporting, including a requirement to report companion animal abuse
Creates provisions relating to over-the-counter medications
Establishes and modifies provisions relating to child abuse or neglect
Grants use of the House Chamber to Rockwood South Middle School on April 14, 2025, from 1:00 p.m. to 2:00 p.m. for presentation of the Glory Awards

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Safe Place for Newborns Fund" for the installation of newborn safety incubators
Modifies standards for storage and use of gold and silver
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Prohibits the use of SNAP benefits to purchase certain foods
